# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF INFLUENCE: Make Art Not Content*

- id: 9022531
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9022531

## videos

- `iframe` https://dialog.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://www.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/controller-with-preconnect-5923ab14c9a2faa0ad2f0f95c16ea979.html#__shared_params__[version]=v3&__shared_params__[light_experiment_assignments]=%7B%22token%22%3A%224a04a90f-1c44-40f3-8324-15611ed50afc%22%2C%22assignments%22%3A%7B%22link_ewcs_prewarm_experiment_v2%22%3A%22control%22%7D%7D&apiKey=pk_live_hkgvgSBxG4TAl3zGlXiB1KUX&stripeJsId=4a04a90f-1c44-40f3-8324-15611ed50afc&stripeObjId=sobj-109d00cb-c2d7-423c-930c-a6c1036cd650&firstStripeInstanceCreatedLatency=362&controllerCount=1&isCheckout=false&stripeJsLoadTime=1778072665653&manualBrowserDeprecationRollout=false&mids[guid]=NA&mids[muid]=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&mids[sid]=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&referrer=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F9022531&controllerId=__privateStripeController2961
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F9022531&title=3.%20Every%20Brand%20Should%20Have%20A%20Muse%20(Introducing%20Psychographics)%20%7C%20The&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    3\. Every Brand Should Have A Muse (Introducing Psychographics) 

Photographers shoot models. That's what they do.

But SOME photographers are lucky enough to have a muse. This muse is what inspires their best work.

**Every business should have a muse too: their loyal customers.**

Call them your audience, fans, clients, avatar, guests, patients... Whatever you like.

But for simplicity here, I'll refer to them as your "audience".

You should understand and love your audience. They give you life.

> **“You have to be religious about your audience.” Gary V**

My first year working with The Weeknd was all about the fans. We even started a website for them... about them. We spent 2-3 hours after every show doing meet and greets and autograph signings. 

Serve your audience like that.

Don't worry about your "followers".

Your followers are merely people that have "collected you" over time. When it's time for their end-of-year social media clean up, you might be gone like a fad.

But within all your followers is a devoted audience. They're the people who LOOK for your posts every day. 

They're the ones who would notice if you were gone. 

_Focus on THEM._

Do you want to go viral?

Trick question.

**You don't actually want to go viral. That's for attracting the masses. Tons of the wrong people.**

The last thing you want to do is attract everybody. Especially by trying to appeal to _everybody_. Homogenizing yourself for those who only come to “check you out” doesn't help you in the long run. Those are the passer-bys... the window shoppers. [Play for your "true fans" and they'll tell your FUTURE "true fans" about you.](https://kk.org/thetechnium/1000-true-fans/)

And yes, if you do this well, you'll get haters.

Trust me... 

_Haters are your second-best spokespeople._

My dad always says, “For every person who hates what you do... there's someone who loves it BECAUSE the other person hates it.” 

The noise the haters make to criticize you will be the best PR to attract even more of “your people”.

If you want to grow faster than everyone else you have to ignore all the stuff that everyone else is obsessed about. All of that just feeds your ego and you just do it for outside perception. Obsess about your audience instead. Do more of what's foundational. Add value to your customer's lives and you win. Even if you're not on Snapchat or Fakebook or a part of the Instagram mafia. 

But let's dig deeper here. 

Do you even know who "your people" are?

So many businesses think it's their "customer demographic".

Guys, we're not in the MySpace era. 

Today, in order for you to use all these digital marketing tools more effectively... you're going to have to wrap your head around something more powerful: _psychographics._

  


## Psychographics

Ok. Before we get all poetic about it, you should answer basic demographic questions. You should know things about your audience like: gender, where they're from, their race, religion etc... 

Don't be afraid to EXCLUDE. You want to EXCLUDE. Who's your audience? Men or women? Yeah, yeah, I know it's both. But who is it REALLY (it doesn't mean others won't like it -- I'm asking you who are you making it for?) Narrow it down. Are you making this for older people or younger people? Urban people or country people.

Next, you figure out the psychographics of your audience. In todays world psychographics are MORE important than demographics.

Demographics is data on things you can see.

**Psychographics is about knowing how your audience thinks and feels.**

To figure out your audience's psychographics, you need to engage in a little method acting. Imagine "your people" and then you imagine what they want. What websites do they go to online? What food do they eat? Are they spiritual? Do they like video games? What kind of causes would they march for? You should know what they dream of and what would make their day. 

And then from now on, when you communicate with them... Do it on their level. Talk to them in the way that you KNOW that they'd be most receptive... every single time.

You need to know your audience better than they know themselves. 

You need to be like Bill Clinton back in '92 fighting to win the presidency.

"I feel your painnnnnn..."

Find 10 people to create for. Perfect your craft until they ALL wanna scream and tell everybody they know about you! 

_Those people will lead you to the next 100... and then the next 1000…_

**I mean...If you can't attract ten die-hards then how are you planning to rule the world?**

When communication is tailor-made for "your people" you pretty much never have to hype anything or use manipulation “tactics“ like the others do. 

Hearing from you... becomes (gasp) desirable!

You become your own "word of mouth". They trust you THAT much.

If you don’t believe me read the Bezos letters.

Did you know that Jeff Bezos (the owner of Amazon) wrote an [end of year email to all his employees detailing his complete vision for the future... every year since the beginning](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1SpgDsIpC_cAS0O4cBz4Sb_GJcEIBhUtA/view)?

Do you realize that everything he wrote came true? 

Guess what the core principle of all those emails were? 

That everything you do-- you need to do it for your audience. They are the Polaris Star.

Not in that cheesy McDonalds way that "the customer is always right".

But in the progressive way that even if the customer is wrong... How can we help them out? 'Cause it could have been us on the other side.

_And I don’t know about you, but from where I sit Amazon looks to be doing alright._

So even if you didn't have a message, just your sheer obsession and dedication to serving your audience with value could make you successful at what you do. But the only problem with that is that it leaves you vulnerable in your niche to someone who is just as dedicated as you... but HAS a message.

The person, brand, company who has a message and makes it resonate with their psychographic is unstoppable. 

All the insanely successful brands and artists out there are doing just that... by design... or by luck.

**Know your audience. Ignore the rest.**

[ Complete and Continue   ]()

I love the part about not wanting to go viral, it's much more important to grow dedicated listeners/fans, and to know who your audience is and also who isn't.

Jonas Paulo

Awaiting Review 

2 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9022531/comments/23354485)

"Know your audience. Ignore the rest." - Among so many BANGERS I already heard on TMS, this one was one I didn't expect! I am always so very concerned about people's opinion about me, about my productions, my "reputation", and my artistic range. But it makes total sense: MY PEOPLE will get it because it is for THEM and for them alone! And if haters hate me and get vocal about it they will only make me known to people they reach and I don't! It's a win-win situation! Father, forgive for I have sinned! I've been forgetful of my audience every once in a while and tried to please everyone... Never again!

Do you have any examples of artists and descriptions of their brands? For example the Nike "Just Do It' post makes sense for a slogan for a company... and I understand operating as such more or less however, what would you say is Drake, J.Cole, Russ, Benny Blanco, Ryan Tedder's message... as a musician... and a brand too... I know why I make music but don't quite have the audience that a well established artist co-sign or not has... like what would Roddy Rich's message/audience be? Or Jeremy Zucker or Baby Keem?

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9022531/comments/15946880)

I've made a list of the top ten people right now that are die hard fans but there are still a couple of close friends on the list and family friends. I feel very grateful to these people for supporting me and being excited about what I'm up to but I am confused about how to bring them all together for the psychographic deep dive. There are demographics that I THINK I want to be talking to, that I think would really appreciate the authentic me, but if they aren't there yet they don't count, right? And if who I have now is a very divided list of 28-35 yr olds and then a group of people who are 60+, who are on both coasts, who represent different income levels and have super different beliefs who should I focus on? Even if I just make it a matter of who has bought my art before I get a pretty diverse list - which again I'm so thankful for.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

![Father Bronques](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/6ab90ae46a200706e774b0b9fbeb0e8d?d=mm&s=70) Instructor 

Father Bronques

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/9022531/comments/15978628)

Two things SaraMarie: 1\. You should use non family, non friends to figure out your psychographic. Choose people who have invested time and or money in your art with no other vested interest. They are the purest fan. 2\. If they seem all over the place, it's an illusion. Do market research. ASK them and you'll see that a weed smoking teenager and a hi-strung entrepreneur may like the same things about what you create.
